quotes.text: First-level thinking says, 'It's a good company; let's buy the stock.' Second-level thinking says, 'It's a good company, but everyone thinks it's a great company, and it's not. So the stock's overrated and overpriced; let's sell.'
Rule number one: Most things will prove to be cyclical. Rule number two: Some of the greatest opportunities for gain and loss come when other people forget rule number one.
To achieve superior results, you have to hold non-consensus views regarding value, and they have to be accurate.
Risk means more things can happen than will happen.

The mood swings of the securities markets resemble the movement of a pendulum. Although the midpoint of its arc best describes the location of the pendulum 'on average,' it actually spends very little of its time there.
What the wise man does in the beginning, the fool does in the end.
The goal is to win more when you're right than you lose when you're wrong. It's about asymmetry.
The best opportunities come in times of maximum pessimism.
My approach is to look for asymmetric investments where the downside is limited but the upside is significant.
I was convinced the world was wrong, and I was right. That's not arrogance, that's just what I believed based on the data.
Bull markets are born on pessimism, grow on skepticism, mature on optimism, and die on euphoria.
The only way to avoid mistakes is to not invest, which is the biggest mistake of all.
